markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Plan bounded graph traversals with edge-type allowlists, depth caps, hub suppression, and actor-visible rationale. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: GraphTraversalPlanner leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: GraphTraversalPlanner is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified 924dc6bc; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

findings.title: N+1 DB round-trips per traversal and uncapped edge vector
findings.evidence: graph_planner.rs:168,190 call entity_edge_degree + list_knowledge_edges_for_entity once per visited node (<=2*max_nodes=128 queries/traversal); edges (line 202) are appended with no max_candidates_total cap (a node with degree<=hub_suppression can add up to ~50 edges).
findings.fix: Batch the per-node degree/edge reads (single query over the frontier) and cap edges at budgets.max_candidates_total.

adversarial_review_v2.note: Bounded BFS proven over a real edge (relationship_id cited) + allowlist exclusion proven (pipeline_tests:140,182); deterministic frontier + edge ordering.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-132 requires a bounded, explainable graph-traversal planner over the project knowledge graph (edge-type allowlist, depth/node caps, hub suppression, deterministic relationship_id citations) with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority. The committed graph_planner.rs implements exactly this as a real bounded BFS over the native knowledge_edges table, proven by real-PostgreSQL integration tests (non-empty candidates over a real edge, relationship_id citation, allowlist exclusion) plus an end-to-end executed graph_traversal path that persists a replayable trace. Spec-aligned to current v02.194.
findings: Implementation commit 924dc6bc adds src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_retrieval/graph_planner.rs (289 lines): GraphTraversalPolicy (edge_type_allowlist, max_depth=2, max_nodes=64, hub_degree_suppression=50) + GraphTraversalPlanner.traverse() doing a real bounded BFS. All four MT bounding controls present and actor-visible in GraphTraversalResult (visited, edges, suppressed_hubs, stop_reason).

Deterministic as required: frontier seeded in sorted order (BTreeSet), neighbor edges sorted by relationship_id (graph_planner.rs:189), final edges sorted+deduped by (relationship_id, edge_id) (graph_planner.rs:213-219). cited_relationship_ids() returns sorted/deduped ids for replayable trace citation.

sub_rule_1 PASS: grep of graph_planner.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/placeholder returned no matches. No deferred-live escape on the required path.

sub_rule_2 PASS (real native resource touch): planner runs over the real Handshake PostgreSQL knowledge_edges graph via db.list_knowledge_edges_for_entity (storage/knowledge.rs:2844/4220) and entity_edge_degree (storage/knowledge_memory.rs:1284, a real SQL COUNT over knowledge_edges excluding retired). Tests knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests.rs:292-330 and 334-363 create real entities + a real edge via upsert_knowledge_edge against the managed-PG MemoryFixture, run traverse(), and assert candidates, relationship_id citation, and allowlist exclusion. skip_if_no_pg (line 61) SKIPs loudly when PG absent with NO SQLite/mock fallback; pool_for (knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:146) opens a real PgPool into an isolated schema.

End-to-end native proof: executed_pipeline_graph_path_ranks_and_compiles (pipeline_tests.rs:610) drives the executor through graph_traversal mode and asserts traces[0].retrieval_mode == 'graph_traversal' (line 689) persisted in PG. Module wired into the crate (knowledge_retrieval/mod.rs:41 pub mod graph_planner; lib.rs:207 pub mod knowledge_retrieval).

Spec alignment: graph_traversal is an explicit cheapest-authoritative retrieval mode in current Master Spec v02.194 (02-system-architecture.md:2817, 11526 'graph_traversal SHOULD be preferred over hybrid_rag when Loom or Knowledge Graph neighborhoods can satisfy the task within budgets'). Implementation matches.

Non-blocking perf nit only (matches adversarial-review LOW): one entity_edge_degree + one list_knowledge_edges_for_entity DB call per visited node (graph_planner.rs:168/190, bounded by 2*max_nodes), and the edges vector has no max_candidates_total cap beyond hub suppression. Bounded and correct; a batching/cap optimization, not a correctness or realism failure.

Aside (non-blocking per ground rules): MT-132 mt_id collides with an unrelated WP-KERNEL-005 diagnostics commit d2a41795; the WP-KERNEL-009 graph-planner commit is 924dc6bc as declared. Paperwork concern only.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
